questions.text: A public S3 bucket listing contains customer medical record filenames. What is the IMMEDIATE priority?

options.text: Delete logs to save storage costs
Block public access, preserve logs, and initiate breach assessment per policy
Post an apology tweet before containment
Rename the bucket and keep it public

questions.explanation: Exposure of health data triggers containment and regulated breach workflows.

questions.text: DLP alerts on a spreadsheet labeled 'Internal' leaving via personal webmail. Best FIRST step?

options.text: Publish the file on the intranet
Fire the user automatically
Disable DLP to reduce noise
Confirm classification, block transmission if policy requires, and interview user with HR guidance

questions.explanation: Validate sensitivity and policy, then respond proportionally with HR/legal as needed.

questions.text: Git history contains AWS keys for a retired project. What reduces future sensitive exposure?

options.text: Make the repo private without rotation
Add a README saying 'do not use keys'
Rotate keys, purge secrets from history, and enable secret scanning on all repos
Ignore because the project is old

questions.explanation: Secrets in VCS remain reachable until rotated and removed; scanning prevents recurrence.

questions.text: During a tabletop on sensitive data exposure, leadership asks for a measurable KPI. Which metric fits BEST?

options.text: Total pages printed in color only
Documented incidents or control failures tied to sensitive data exposure trending down quarter over quarter
Number of marketing emails sent
Average desk chair height

questions.explanation: Effective sensitive data exposure programs use measurable outcomes, not vanity metrics.

questions.text: An audit sampling sensitive data exposure controls finds inconsistent enforcement between sites. What should you standardize FIRST?

options.text: Allow each site to opt out silently
Remove all logging to reduce findings
Disable training to save time
Baseline configuration and mandatory procedures referenced in policy with periodic attestation

questions.explanation: Consistency requires written baselines and attestations for sensitive data exposure.

questions.text: Laptops are stolen from a locked office; drives use BitLocker with TPM+PIN. Which data state is MOST protected?

options.text: Printed documents in recycling
Data in use while logged in and unlocked
Data in transit inside the LAN only
Data at rest on disk while powered off

questions.explanation: Full-disk encryption protects at rest when off; logged-in sessions remain exposed.

questions.text: A team proposes TLS 1.0 for legacy clients on an internet-facing API. What is the BEST security stance?

options.text: Enable SSLv3 for compatibility
Use self-signed certs without rotation
Terminate TLS at the user's browser only
Deprecate TLS 1.0/1.1 and document exceptions with compensating controls and timelines

questions.explanation: Legacy TLS is broken; phased migration beats indefinite weak protocols on public APIs.

questions.text: Database backups are copied to tape unencrypted for 'speed.' What risk should leadership accept if continuing?

options.text: Physical loss of tapes exposes all backup contents without key compromise
Cloud backups are automatically encrypted at rest always
Encryption slows restores so it is never needed
No risk if tapes stay in the server room

questions.explanation: Offline media theft bypasses network controls; encryption at rest for backups is standard.

questions.text: During a tabletop on encryption, leadership asks for a measurable KPI. Which metric fits BEST?

options.text: Documented incidents or control failures tied to encryption trending down quarter over quarter
Average desk chair height
Total pages printed in color only
Number of marketing emails sent

questions.explanation: Effective encryption programs use measurable outcomes, not vanity metrics.

questions.text: An audit sampling encryption controls finds inconsistent enforcement between sites. What should you standardize FIRST?

options.text: Disable training to save time
Baseline configuration and mandatory procedures referenced in policy with periodic attestation
Remove all logging to reduce findings
Allow each site to opt out silently

questions.explanation: Consistency requires written baselines and attestations for encryption.

questions.text: DLP blocks a nurse emailing treatment notes to a personal account for 'convenience.' Which outcome is correct?

options.text: Block stands; offer approved secure transfer methods
Forward blocked content to IT via CC
Disable DLP for clinical staff
Allow if the user is senior

questions.explanation: HIPAA-aligned environments require controlling PHI egress; approved tools replace personal email.

questions.text: Endpoint DLP generates many false positives on source code zips. What tuning is BEST?

options.text: Scope policies to labeled repositories and exfil channels, not all zip creation
Turn off endpoint DLP entirely
Alert on every file save
Whitelist all developers permanently

questions.explanation: Effective DLP targets high-risk egress with context to limit alert fatigue.

questions.text: A contractor pastes customer PII into a public ChatGPT session. Which combined control helps MOST?

options.text: Block all HTTPS
Remove contractor laptops
Disable spelling checker
Browser DLP, acceptable-use policy, and enterprise AI gateway with logging

questions.explanation: GenAI paste is a new exfil path; technical and contractual controls together reduce risk.

questions.text: During a tabletop on data loss prevention (dlp), leadership asks for a measurable KPI. Which metric fits BEST?

options.text: Average desk chair height
Total pages printed in color only
Number of marketing emails sent
Documented incidents or control failures tied to data loss prevention (dlp) trending down quarter over quarter

questions.explanation: Effective data loss prevention (dlp) programs use measurable outcomes, not vanity metrics.

questions.text: An audit sampling data loss prevention (dlp) controls finds inconsistent enforcement between sites. What should you standardize FIRST?

options.text: Disable training to save time
Baseline configuration and mandatory procedures referenced in policy with periodic attestation
Remove all logging to reduce findings
Allow each site to opt out silently

questions.explanation: Consistency requires written baselines and attestations for data loss prevention (dlp).

questions.text: USB storage is disabled, but staff use phone charging cables that mount as MTP devices. What should you add?

options.text: Allow all USB if labeled with asset tags
Disable only Wi-Fi instead
Remove antivirus to improve speed
Device control policies blocking unauthorized portable storage classes

questions.explanation: Charging-only vs data-capable USB requires device control, not just drive letters.

questions.text: A found USB drive is plugged into a SOC analyst machine 'to see who lost it.' What is the SAFE procedure?

options.text: Insert into the domain controller to scan faster
Email the files to the whole team
Analyze only in a dedicated malware sandbox VM disconnected from production
Open on the analyst daily workstation

questions.explanation: Unknown USBs are bait; sandbox isolation prevents accidental infection.

questions.text: Marketing requests USB giveaways with preloaded brochures. Security concern to raise?

options.text: USBs are always read-only
HIPAA requires USB giveaways
Supply-chain integrity—USBs could be tampered before distribution
Brochures cannot contain malware

questions.explanation: Preloaded media is a supply-chain risk; sourced devices and hashing matter.

questions.text: During a tabletop on removable media, leadership asks for a measurable KPI. Which metric fits BEST?

options.text: Documented incidents or control failures tied to removable media trending down quarter over quarter
Number of marketing emails sent
Total pages printed in color only
Average desk chair height

questions.explanation: Effective removable media programs use measurable outcomes, not vanity metrics.

questions.text: An audit sampling removable media controls finds inconsistent enforcement between sites. What should you standardize FIRST?

options.text: Disable training to save time
Baseline configuration and mandatory procedures referenced in policy with periodic attestation
Remove all logging to reduce findings
Allow each site to opt out silently

questions.explanation: Consistency requires written baselines and attestations for removable media.

questions.text: Ransomware encrypts production; backups exist but are on the same VLAN as servers. Likely failure mode?

options.text: Tape libraries on VLAN are immune without testing
Backups automatically decrypt files
Ransomware deletes or encrypts online-connected backup shares
Snapshots cannot be targeted

questions.explanation: Immutable or offline backups are required; network-local copies often die with production.

questions.text: Restore test last ran three years ago. Which risk is MOST acute?

options.text: Backups run too frequently
RPO is automatically zero
Backups may be corrupt or procedures untested when needed
Encryption is impossible on tape

questions.explanation: Untested backups are a common disaster recovery gap.

questions.text: A cloud admin can delete production and backup vaults with one role. Best fix?

options.text: Store backups only on the admin laptop
One super-admin for speed
Disable logging on backup API
Separate backup admin role with MFA, immutability, and break-glass monitoring

questions.explanation: Backup integrity requires separation of duties and immutability features.

questions.text: During a tabletop on backups, leadership asks for a measurable KPI. Which metric fits BEST?

options.text: Documented incidents or control failures tied to backups trending down quarter over quarter
Total pages printed in color only
Number of marketing emails sent
Average desk chair height

questions.explanation: Effective backups programs use measurable outcomes, not vanity metrics.

questions.text: An audit sampling backups controls finds inconsistent enforcement between sites. What should you standardize FIRST?

options.text: Allow each site to opt out silently
Baseline configuration and mandatory procedures referenced in policy with periodic attestation
Remove all logging to reduce findings
Disable training to save time

questions.explanation: Consistency requires written baselines and attestations for backups.
